title: The Grain Road
timelinePosition: Opens immediately after the Chapter 4 decision to leave the sick village and pursue the source southwest.
openLocation: Lordaeron's trade-road network between village depots and the roads feeding Andorhal.
transport: Mounted travel on Thalassian horses between depots, inns, and granary stops.
immediateObjective: Trace the contaminated grain backward through records, depots, and delivery routes until the supply chain narrows to a source hub.
constraints: No proof of Barov complicity before stronger evidence; the name enters as paperwork pressure, not solved guilt.
No trust or softness overstatement — the naming shift to 'Jaina' is functional rhythm, not tenderness.
The old seller remains an unresolved memory, not an identified threat.

povKnowsAtOpen: Organized hands are seeding the grain roads and watching what happens.
The village-level watcher died by poison before he could be questioned.
The strongest route logic points southwest toward Andorhal.
Millhaven remains the first fully claimed village; most other settlements are still only in confused early-stage sickness.
Jaina's sensitivity is putting them ahead of public understanding, but not ahead of the rollout itself; they are still following grain that has already landed.
Jaina believes exposure depth and repeated ingestion matter, not merely presence versus absence.
Millhaven left two weak grain-side anomalies worth filing for later: the old seller on the north road and the survivor girl's memory of a newer grain man.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Who owns or controls the roads and storehouses they are tracing.
Whether the Barovs are architects, collaborators, or unwitting hosts.
Whether the old grain seller from Chapter 3 belongs in the same picture.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No certainty about Barov complicity before the paperwork says so.
No trust language stronger than grim working reliance.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: The southern feeder road toward Andorhal is the chosen route.
The Barov name now sits in the grain paperwork, but not yet as proven guilt.
Arthas's active military theater lies near the road they have to ride.
The old seller is worth remembering, but still not identified.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Sylvanas is still telling herself this is a southern extension of border work, even with every mile proving otherwise.
pov.surfaceObjective: Turn scattered sickness sites and grain records into a usable road map that points toward the source.
pov.pressureSource: The farther south they ride, the less this looks like something she can bracket as Lordaeron's mess, and the road keeps rewarding Jaina's pattern read.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas still treats Jaina's work like extra help riding under her command, even when the mage is the one keeping the route coherent.
pov.chapterTest: The paper trail keeps changing shape fast enough that Sylvanas has to trust Jaina's live revisions instead of waiting for field confirmation of everything.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep treating Jaina as support she brings along, or start moving as if the mage's read sets direction as much as her own.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close Sylvanas is no longer merely bringing Jaina along. She is riding with her on purpose.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 6 opens with Sylvanas consulting Jaina as part of the method, not as an afterthought.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The woman who can keep the pattern straight while it changes under them.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Useful, but still inclined to see the map cleaner than the world ever is.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Jaina adjusts fast, drops bad assumptions without vanity, and keeps the road legible in motion. That discipline makes Sylvanas trust the read more than the mage's rank or theory.
throughPov.interactionRule: This is where Sylvanas can think Jaina's name without it feeling like a special event. Do not make it tender. Just natural.

team.label: partnership
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: They stop working in turns and start working in rhythm. One woman finds the route, the other keeps it moving.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Sylvanas's internal register loosens a notch. Respect is easier than saying so.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Reliance is beginning. Trust is still conditional and tactical.

entries.rule: Already gone north with the warning relay and the survivor girl; do not imply her presence in this block.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 6 opens on the same four-person riding party taking the southern feeder road that will bring them near Strahnbrad before the final push on Andorhal.

scenes.title: The Paper Trail
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn one dead village and a string of ordinary-looking sickness sites into a road map, and reveal how big the thing really is.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Grain is carrying it; southwest is where the road keeps pulling them.
scenes.arcPressure: The wider the route becomes, the harder it is for Sylvanas to treat this as somebody else's southern problem; the paper trail keeps proving that Jaina's pattern read and Sylvanas's route logic only work when used together.

enrichment.detail: Andorhal was Lordaeron's main agricultural distribution center east of Lordamere Lake, and infected grain moving out along the King's Road reached towns such as Hearthglen and Stratholme. That supports this chapter's widening ledger trail as the kingdom's ordinary food system turning into the weapon.

Canon frames Sylvanas as Ranger-General of Silvermoon and a front-line commander, so treating manifests, wagon marks, and feeder roads like troop movement is character-grounded rather than an invented detective skill spike.

beats.goal: Track contaminated deliveries using records rather than contamination alone.
beats.conflict: The truth is buried in ordinary paperwork — invoices, loading tallies, inn ledgers, delivery marks.

outcome.summary: The route begins to resolve, but only by proving the problem is much bigger than one or two villages.

beats.valueShift: known carrier -> the road is bigger than they thought
newOnPageInformation: Millhaven and multiple mildly sick settlements share the same supply pattern.
Most sites still read as ordinary illness to the people living in them.
Severity tracks how long a place depends on the shipped grain, not just whether contamination is present at all.
Sylvanas reads wagon routes the way she reads troop movement.
Jaina's contamination notes become more useful when tied to route records.
Jaina changes her read as fast as the numbers change, which Sylvanas recognizes as discipline rather than fussiness.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who controls the route at origin.
sensoryAnchors: dusty account books
grain tallies and wagon marks
horses tied outside while the party works inside paper-heavy rooms
beats.transitionIntent: trail-pressure: the record trail leads them to a depot where the evidence should be thicker.
beats.verifierNotes: Grain as carrier and Jaina's exposure-depth model are already established. Treat this as the investigation using those answers at scale, not rediscovering them.

beats.goal: Determine whether this is local sabotage or something using the whole grain road.
beats.conflict: Every additional ledger points outward to more villages, more roads, more deliveries.

outcome.summary: The scale is confirmed as regional, and that means they are not chasing a village trick but something big enough to lean on the kingdom's grain roads.

beats.valueShift: the road is bigger than they thought -> somebody has turned food into a regional weapon
newOnPageInformation: The same grain road touches dozens of settlements.
This is not one bad chain but a kingdom-wide food road.
sensoryAnchors: repeating place names
Jaina's map and Sylvanas's route marks overlaying each other
the sick feeling of a pattern getting cleaner as the stakes get worse
beats.transitionIntent: enemy-pressure: the next depot should confirm the route, unless the enemy is already reacting to them.
beats.verifierNotes: The regional scale is the new turn. The existence of an organized network is already established by the granary watcher and poison protocol.

beats.dilemma: Keep treating the trail as static evidence, or assume the conspiracy is already moving to erase it.
beats.decision: Assume an active opponent and press harder.
beats.valueShift: somebody has turned food into a regional weapon -> live enemy
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas no longer treats the investigation as passive archaeology.
The party begins expecting resistance ahead.
Their lead is real but narrow: they are seeing the network early, never early enough to get in front of a shipment.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How close the enemy is staying to them in real time.
sensoryAnchors: evening road
tired horses
Cyndia and Lyriel watching the perimeter while the leads work
beats.transitionIntent: depot-test: the next depot tests whether the enemy is already ahead of them.
beats.verifierNotes: Assuming a live opponent grows directly out of chapter4's watcher-and-silencing proof. Do not frame this as a brand-new suspicion lane.

scenes.title: The Cleaned Depot
scenes.sceneFunction: Prove the enemy is reacting and narrow the road to a noble-house line.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: They are now hunting a live network, not just following dead evidence.
scenes.arcPressure: The cleaned depot forces Sylvanas to work from fragments and accept Jaina's magical read as part of the same hunt, because waiting for cleaner field certainty only means arriving after the enemy again.

enrichment.detail: House Barov is an extremely wealthy Lordaeron family with holdings tied to Caer Darrow, Brill, Southshore, and Tarren Mill. That makes the Barov name credible as land-and-logistics pressure on the paperwork before it becomes proof of direct guilt.

Canon later ties the Barovs to Kel'Thuzad and Scholomance beneath Caer Darrow; this chapter should preserve uncertainty by treating the paperwork link as suspicious infrastructure overlap, not as solved complicity. Kept as deliberate story-stage caution, not contradiction.

beats.goal: Seize the next depot's records before they disappear.
beats.conflict: The depot has already been cleaned: records stripped, grain moved, evidence erased.

outcome.summary: They arrive at the right place and discover they were anticipated.

beats.valueShift: live enemy -> the enemy is erasing the trail
newOnPageInformation: Someone is clearing sites ahead of the investigation.
The conspiracy has enough awareness to react to their movement.
sensoryAnchors: empty shelves where ledgers should be
fresh absence rather than old ruin
warehouse quiet that feels recently manufactured
beats.transitionIntent: body-pressure: if the paperwork is gone, the body may still speak.
beats.verifierNotes: The cleaned depot escalates the established network into counter-investigation. It is not first proof that someone is steering the spread.

beats.goal: Learn what happened to the man who should have held the books.
beats.conflict: The overseer looks naturally dead until Jaina reads the same precise necromantic touch used elsewhere.

outcome.summary: They gain confirmation of targeted silencing and lose the only local witness who might have named names.

beats.valueShift: the enemy is erasing the trail -> the enemy kills to preserve the trail
newOnPageInformation: This was not random death.
The conspiracy can deploy precise, limited magical violence as well as mass poisoning.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The exact structure linking depots to the source hub.
sensoryAnchors: body in an office that should have been occupied
wrong residue beneath an apparently clean death
Jaina reading magic where Sylvanas reads staging
beats.transitionIntent: fragment-pressure: they now search for surviving scraps, partial ledgers, and route marks.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina already knows necromancy in the broad sense. The escalation here is repeatable silencing method and deliberate cleanup, not necromancy's existence.

beats.goal: Recover enough of the route map to identify the upstream owner or network.
beats.conflict: Only fragments remain, and Sylvanas has to reconstruct the route from pieces instead of whole books.

outcome.summary: The surviving fragments point to Barov grain operations, but ownership does not yet equal guilt.

beats.valueShift: the enemy kills to preserve the trail -> the road narrows to Barov grain
newOnPageInformation: Barov holdings sit inside the same road-and-storehouse web.
Andorhal is the place where that road web pinches together.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the Barovs are complicit or penetrated.
sensoryAnchors: torn route sheet
House seal surviving in wax
Sylvanas reconstructing from scraps rather than complete records
beats.transitionIntent: choice-pressure: the party now has a route name, a hub, and a reason to choose its next road carefully.
beats.verifierNotes: The Barov name enters as paperwork pressure inside the route, not proven guilt. Keep infrastructure overlap and culpability separate.

scenes.title: Night Analysis and the South Road
scenes.sceneFunction: Let the name change happen quietly and choose the road into Chapter 6.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Barov infrastructure points toward Andorhal; the enemy is erasing evidence ahead of them.
scenes.arcPressure: Choosing the south road means Sylvanas has to admit Jaina's live revisions are now part of how she moves, even as the route drags them toward Arthas's war-country instead of a cleaner, safer chase.

enrichment.detail: Strahnbrad sits in the northeastern foothills of the Alterac Mountains near the Western Plaguelands border, east of Alterac and west of Chillwind Point. That makes a southern feeder road angling past it toward Andorhal feel geographically grounded rather than convenient.

Jaina is canonically Antonidas's apprentice and an early plague investigator, so her fast revisions to the contamination model should read as disciplined field intelligence rather than abstract theory. This chapter's shift is not that she becomes smarter, but that Sylvanas starts moving on her read in real time.

beats.goal: Reconcile Jaina's contamination model with the route fragments.
beats.conflict: At this point the work is too mixed together for Sylvanas to keep thinking of Jaina only as 'the mage.'

outcome.summary: The analysis coheres cleanly enough to set the road choice, and Sylvanas's internal naming changes without ceremony.

beats.valueShift: the route narrows to Barov roads -> reliance made plain
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas now thinks 'Jaina' naturally.
Jaina stops trimming her explanations once she sees Sylvanas can follow them.
Jaina can now sketch the carrier, the depth of exposure, and the way sickness worsens, even if the last step still escapes her.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: The degree of Barov knowledge or guilt.
sensoryAnchors: candlelight over ledgers and contamination map
quiet room with Cyndia and Lyriel taking perimeter turns outside
the name landing internally without fanfare
beats.transitionIntent: memory-pressure: with their working rhythm established, the old trader detail resurfaces with more bite.
beats.verifierNotes: Sylvanas's internal use of Jaina marks operational respect and shared method. Do not write it as tenderness or a sudden trust collapse.
beats.verifierModification: Changed the outcome beat from yes-but to yes so the chapter gets one cleaner local gain after the cleaned-depot run and the arc shift lands with more force.

beats.dilemma: File the old grain seller as one more road contact, or give that memory more weight now that grain is confirmed as the thing carrying it.
beats.decision: File it harder, but do not divert the route yet.
newOnPageInformation: The empty grain wagon from Chapter 3 matters more than it did then.
Sylvanas's memory is beginning to revisit previously unremarkable details.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the trader belongs to the conspiracy or merely moved through its roads.
sensoryAnchors: grain dust memory in old wagon slats
helpful voice recalled without a face that holds
annoyance at not having acted on something she could not yet have known to act on
beats.transitionIntent: route-choice: they now choose the actual road into Chapter 6.
beats.verifierNotes: This remains a filed memory and unresolved clue. Do not identify the trader, upgrade him to a confirmed threat, or write Sylvanas as having missed something obvious.

beats.goal: Select the fastest usable route into Andorhal's network without riding blind into the worst contamination corridor.
beats.conflict: The cleanest surviving paperwork and troop traffic point to the southern feeder road that joins the Andorhal approach near Strahnbrad, not the most direct plague-saturated line.

outcome.summary: They now have a practical route, but that route intersects Prince Arthas's current campaign zone.

beats.valueShift: unresolved suspicion -> the road to Andorhal now runs through human war-country
newOnPageInformation: The Chapter 6 Strahnbrad encounter is a consequence of the chosen route, not a random detour.
The party is following an active road used by both grain traffic and responding human forces.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas will help or hinder.
sensoryAnchors: road map weighted by knife and candle stub
horse lines being checked for a dawn departure
the route physically angling toward both Strahnbrad and Andorhal
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-hook: Chapter 6 opens on this road with the same four-person party.
beats.verifierNotes: The route choice is the payoff of the shared method: Jaina narrows which line is survivable and Sylvanas turns that read into movement. Keep the reliance tactical, not soft.

departuresLocked: The core riding party remains Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel.
Depot clerks, innkeepers, merchants, depot staff, and the dead overseer are local to this chapter's logistics trail only.
factsLockedForChapter6: The party is riding the southern feeder road toward Andorhal.
That road takes them near Strahnbrad and Arthas's active military theater.
The Barov name is now attached to the grain network, but complicity is not yet proven.
The old seller is retrospectively worth remembering, but still not identified.
factsNotYetEarned: Explicit trust language between the leads.
Recognition of the trader as the same threat agent who keeps appearing.

researchSources.label: Warcraft Wiki — Barov
usedFor: Scholomance / Kel'Thuzad tie as future-pressure context

warnings: Beat 1 still opens on records and route logic rather than immediate physical danger, so the prose needs active handling and motion on the page to avoid a summary-feel start.

Beats 1-5 darken in a largely continuous direction. Keep each stage functionally distinct: widening scale, live-opponent realization, cleaned-depot reversal, silenced witness, then Barov paperwork pressure.

Beats 4-7 remain four consecutive scene beats before the sequel at beat 8. Differentiate the depot sequence from the night-analysis scene so the chapter does not flatten into one long procedural block.

The outcome pattern now has one cleaner lift at beat 7, but the chapter still leans hard on pressured gains. Preserve that modulation so the analytical win actually feels like air before the Chapter 6 hook.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter4's southbound decision: the same four-person party leaves the living village with grain confirmed and the granary death still fresh, turning relief work into pursuit by following manifests, depots, and wagon routes southwest toward Andorhal.

craftResearch.findings: Each discovery should change the type or scale of evidence, not repeat the same clue shape. Distinct scene goals keep procedural chapters from feeling like a checklist.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Writers Digest, Mystery and Suspense Magazine

craftResearch.findings: Journey beats stay alive when every leg carries a mini-goal and does more than move bodies through space. Travel should advance plot while sharpening character dynamics or world pressure.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Writing Beginner, Ken Hughes Author

craftResearch.findings: Chapter openings need an active present-tense problem with a clear POV, place, and immediate tension. Procedural setup survives when it begins in motion rather than in detached explanation.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Reedsy, Book Light Editorial

craftResearch.findings: Dense clue clusters need a brief processing or decision pocket afterward. Alternating hard discovery with short recalibration keeps thriller momentum from turning monotonous.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Writers Digest, British Proofreading

craftResearch.findings: End with a sharper road ahead, unanswered pressure, or a new objective created by the evidence rather than a full solution. The close should force the next chapter open.
craftResearch.source: Web craft survey: Book Light Editorial, Writers in the Storm

structure: Beat 1 opens on active pursuit through ledgers, depots, and horse changes rather than recap, but it still needs motion-heavy prose support because the underlying material is procedural.
Scene 1 escalates cleanly by changing evidence type: local manifest comparison becomes kingdom-scale pattern recognition, then becomes active-hunt decision.
Scene 2 is the chapter's midpoint reversal. The cleaned depot turns a paper chase into counterintelligence, and the dead overseer proves the enemy is managing exposure in real time.
Beat 6 gives the chapter its directional narrowing by converting general southwest pressure into Barov-linked infrastructure and a tighter Andorhal pinch point.
Beat 7 needed a cleaner local gain; changing its outcome to yes prevents the chapter's long run of blunted wins from feeling too uniform.
Scene 3 closes correctly on route choice rather than explanation, so the chapter ends by sharpening pursuit into the Strahnbrad-and-Arthas lane instead of pausing at analysis.
character: Every beat remains filterable through Sylvanas's perception: route logic, discipline reads, memory irritants, and command decisions all stay inside her limited-third frame.

Sylvanas is not passive for more than a beat at a time. She chooses records over raw contamination, assumes a live enemy, reconstructs the route from scraps, files the trader clue, and commits the final road choice.
Jaina carries a distinct function in every shared scene: contamination model, revision discipline, magical residue reading, and route-risk narrowing. She never duplicates Sylvanas's tactical lane.
Cyndia and Lyriel stay thin but nonredundant: both remain perimeter and riding-security labor, which is enough for a logistics chapter so long as they do not compete with the investigative spine.
The arc block promises a forced choice about whether Jaina remains support or becomes method, and beats 1, 7, and 9 collectively deliver that promise without overstating emotion.
The name shift is earned as operational ease, not tenderness. That keeps the partnership line aligned with the series arc, which is still in the cold-reliance stage.
continuity: The chapter correctly treats grain as a solved carrier question from chapter4. No beat is allowed to rediscover grain as if the village chapter had not already proved it.
Jaina's exposure-depth and repeated-ingestion logic are established before chapter5 opens, so beat 1 was annotated as evolution rather than new insight.

The organized-network lane is already on the page before this chapter starts. Beats 2 through 5 therefore advance watcher discipline into larger-scale cleanup instead of introducing conspiracy from scratch.
Beat 6 needed a continuity guard because Barov is new paperwork pressure but not new certainty. The note now preserves suspicion without premature solution.
Beat 8 advances the old-seller clue without identifying him. The memory file confirms he was memorable, practical, and unresolved, so the chapter must keep him in that state.
The chapter advances the Andorhal-source-node thread from general destination to a more specific feeder-road approach while still refusing to call Andorhal the proven single source.
transitions: Beat 1's trail-pressure handoff lands in beat 2 because the thicker paperwork naturally expands the local route into a regional pattern.
Beat 2's enemy-pressure handoff lands in beat 3 because the scale revelation immediately raises the question of whether the network is static or actively reacting.
Beat 3's depot-test handoff lands cleanly in beat 4; the cleaned depot is exactly the sort of test the prior decision predicts.
Beat 6's choice-pressure handoff lands in beat 7 because the chapter pauses to reconcile route fragments and contamination logic before committing the next road.
Beat 8's route-choice handoff lands directly in beat 9, where the memory-clue beat converts into an actual feeder-road decision instead of dangling as atmosphere.
A new chapterBridge now ties chapter4's granary decision to chapter5's manifest hunt, so the opening inherits the same four riders, same urgency, and same supply-chain focus without reset.

pacing.notes: The chapter rises from procedural pressure to active-network pressure, peaks in the cleaned-depot sequence, breathes in the night-analysis turn, and closes on a sharpened route hook. The scene-to-sequel ratio is still aggressive at 7 scene beats to 2 sequel beats, but the evidence modes change often enough to keep the investigation from going flat.

structure.notes: The opening stays inside live pursuit, the midpoint reversal lands when the depot is already cleaned, the Barov paperwork narrows the chase, and the close creates the next road instead of solving the case. Beat 7 was adjusted to provide one clearer analytical win and better modulation.

character.notes: Sylvanas drives the chapter while Jaina remains indispensable as a distinct specialist. The chapter earns its promised stance shift by making Jaina's read part of Sylvanas's operating method without falsely accelerating emotional trust.

transitions.notes: All 9 beat-level transitionIntent chains were verified against the next beat or scene. No beat-level intents were missing, no scene-boundary carries needed repair, and a direct-carry chapterBridge was added for the chapter opening.

continuity.notes: All 9 beats are forward-driving evolutions rather than recap-only callbacks. No contradictions were found against the memory indexes or loaded entity files; 4 anti-reintroduction risks were guarded around Jaina's established plague read, the pre-existing network, the Barov suspicion lane, and the unresolved old-seller clue.

modifications.description: Changed beat 7 from a yes-but analytical beat to a clean yes so the chapter gains one firmer local win after the cleaned-depot run and the Jaina-method shift lands with more shape.
